text: No validated criteria-based protocol exists specifically for returning athletes to sprinting following lower extremity injury.
Transitioning back to sport in the terminal phases of rehabilitation is a critical aspect of preparing an athlete for return to full participation.
Return to sprinting progressions should be criteria-based rather than time-based, using objective measures to determine readiness for each progression stage.
Sports rehabilitation professionals often lack objective criteria to determine when an athlete is ready to progress to sprinting activities.
A structured, criteria-based sprinting progression is especially important when rehabilitation visits or financial resources are limited, to maximize outcomes within constraints.
